steps.title: Identify the Causal Evidence Needed
steps.content: Since the goal is to determine whether RSRs caused the changes (not just whether they occurred), the students need to compare Montana to a state without RSRs in the same period.

steps.title: Match to the Choices
steps.content: Comparing both Montana data types (insurer numbers and new-policy premiums) to the same types from a comparable non-RSR state in the same period isolates the RSR effect by revealing whether changes were unique to Montana. Choices A, C, and D lack this control: A compares mismatched policy types (new premiums in Montana vs. existing premiums elsewhere), C compares two Montana periods both under RSRs, and D compares two RSR-implementing states instead of using a control. That matches choice B — B is correct.
